name: Fajitas-on-a-Stick
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 pounds boneless beef top sirloin steak, 1 inch thick
2 green or red bell peppers, quartered
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the bamboo skewers in water for 10 minutes; drain the water.
2. Trim fat from beef steak. Cut steak crosswise into 1/2-inch thick strips.
Thread an equal amount of beef, weaving back and forth, onto each skewer.
3. In a small bowl, combine dressing and lime juice, mixing well. Brush the mixture
onto beef, peppers, and onions.
4. Place vegetables on the grid over medium ash-covered coals; grill peppers
uncovered for 12 to 15 minutes and onions for 15 to 20 minutes or until tender,
turning both once. Approximately 10 minutes before vegetables are done,
move vegetables to the outer edge of the grid. Place beef in the center of the grid;
grill for 8 to 10 minutes for medium rare to medium doneness, turning once.
5. Season beef with salt as desired; remove beef from skewers. Serve beef and
vegetables in tortillas with salsa and garnishes. Another great dish!
Make sure you have normal fajita toppings handy, like lettuce, cheese,
tomatoes, sour cream, olives, etc.
